title: Extension.js CLI Readiness Document
description: Single machine-readable verdict for a CLI build, aggregating Tier-1 (unit), Tier-2 (acceptance e2e) and Tier-3 (stability gates) results. One document per (cliCommit x environment) CI run; the website renders the latest per branch/tag.

schemaVersion.description: Bump on breaking changes to this contract.

meta.description: Identity of the build under judgment. cliCommit + examplesCommit together fix the loose coupling between the two repos.

cliCommit.description: Full SHA in extension-js/extension.js that produced the CLI under test.

cliChannel.description: local = built from the monorepo; the rest = published npm dist-tag (remote/publish-gate runs).

examplesCommit.description: Full SHA in extension-js/examples used for Tier-2/3. PIN THIS — it is the missing link today (nightly clones @main unpinned).

environment.description: What the verdict is conditional on. 'display' is load-bearing: content/newtab batches are known to fail headless (see TESTING_RESULTS.md).

capabilityMatrix.description: Join target from extension-js/examples templates-meta.json. Lets the site render template x capability without re-deriving it. Optional: present it when the run was matrix-wide.

results.description: Every signal as a normalized row. A 'cell' is uniquely identified by (tier, dimension, template?, browser?, surface?).

verdict.description: ready is derived from gatingPolicy applied to results — never hand-set.

ready.description: True iff every result whose dimension is in gatingPolicy.required passed (ignoring rows classified infra-flake per gatingPolicy.flakeHandling).

score.description: Optional weighted pass ratio for the badge gradient.

computedFrom.description: Reference to the gatingPolicy version used, e.g. 'gating-policy@1'.

byDimension.description: Rollup status per dimension key (e.g. reload.full, install.modes).

blockers.description: Human-facing reasons ready=false. Each points at a failing result cell.

gatingPolicy.description: Declarative policy that turns results into a verdict. Versioned so the meaning of 'ready' is auditable over time.

required.description: Dimensions that MUST pass for ready=true.

flakeHandling.description: infra-flake-ignored: rows classified 'infra-flake' don't block ready. strict: any non-pass blocks.

status.description: flake = passed only on retry. Pair with classification to decide gating.

tier.description: Tier-1 unit (this repo), Tier-2 acceptance e2e (examples repo), Tier-3 stability gates (examples repo).

dimension.description: Stable key, e.g. unit.develop, reload.full, install.modes, build.oneRun, create.devWorkflowAll, dev.monorepo, runtime.resolution. See README mapping table.

classification.description: For non-pass rows: is the code wrong (code-red) or did infra misbehave (infra-flake, e.g. headless content-script injection per TESTING_RESULTS.md)?

template.description: Tier-2/3 cells: the example template, if scoped.

surface.description: Maps to the examples Playwright project batches.

source.description: Provenance — which existing job/script produced this row.

metrics.description: Quantitative payload. reload-matrix populates sw/nav; suites populate counts.

expected.description: For reload-matrix: the expected sw/nav, e.g. 'sw=1 nav=0'.
